mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST rise together, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, or the signal reverses direction (predicts negative returns) when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 235.0, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent GDP growth and declining unemployment indicate a synchronized macroeconomic upturn that boosts corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and UNRATE signal loses predictive accuracy when GDP growth decouples from labor market improvements, or the interaction effect yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month retail sales decline exceeding 2.0% signals abrupt consumer demand contraction, which immediately triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances following a >2.0% RSAFS drop, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
